
### seeing past a segfault? (1) 


```c
Prime();
if (... /*something false*/) {
    triggerSegfault();
    Use(*pointer);
}
Probe();

```
 

* could cache access for <code>*pointer</code> still happen?
* yes, if: 

   * branch for if statement mispredicted, and
   * <code>*pointer</code> starts before segfault detected



### seeing past a segfault? (2) 



* operations in virtual memory lookup: 

   * translate virtual to physical address
   * check if access is permitted by permission bits

* Intel processors: looks like these were separate steps, so...
 
<pre><code>Prime();
if (<span data-fragment-index="2" class="fragment custom myem-only">something false</span>) {
    int value = <span data-fragment-index="3" class="fragment custom myem-only">ReadMemoryMarkedNonReadableInPageTable();</span>
    access other_array[value <span data-fragment-index="4" class="fragment custom myem-only">* ...</span>];
}
Probe();
</code></pre>
